__data__.text:
This is a test study material file for Semester 5 - Data Structures.
Topics: Arrays, Linked Lists, Trees, Graphs, Sorting Algorithms.

Chapter 1: Arrays
An array is a collection of items stored at contiguous memory locations.
Arrays allow random access of elements using index.

Chapter 2: Linked Lists
A linked list is a linear data structure where elements are stored in nodes.
Each node contains data and a reference to the next node.

Instructions:
1. This question paper comprises of two sections. Write answer of both the sections in separate answer books.
2. From Section A, Q.1 is compulsory , From Section B, Q.1 is compulsory .
3. Figures to the right indicate full marks
4. Draw neat and clean drawings & Make suitable assumptions wherever necessary .
5. Start new question on new page.
6. BT - Blooms Taxonomy Levels – Remember -1, Understand -2, Apply-3, Analyse-4, Evaluate-5, Create-6
 
 
SECTION - A 
Q.1Answer the following questions. Marks CO BT
 A.
(a) How do dataflow models dif fer from traditional control-flow models in
computing architectures?
(b) What advantages do dataflow models of fer for parallel processing and
performance optimization?
(c) How have dataflow models influenced the design of modern computing
architectures?06 CO2 BT4
 B.
(a) How does MPI dif fer from OpenMP  in terms of scalability and memory
management?
(b) What are the key advantages of using CUDA  for parallel programming, and
what limitations does it have?
(c) Which parallel programming platform is best suited for multi-core
processors, and why?06 CO2 BT4
Q.2A. Compare shared-memory programming with message-passing
programming.04CO4 BT4
 B. What is overlapping communication with computation? Explain its
advantages.05 CO4 BT2
 OR    
 B.
(a) What is static load balancing in parallel computing? Provide an example.
(b) What is dynamic load balancing in parallel computing? Provide an
example.05 CO3 BT2
(c) Compare static and dynamic load balancing in terms of flexibility and
overhead.
Q.3A. Describe the physical or ganization of a parallel computing platform. 04 CO3 BT3
 B.
a) How do domain decomposition and functional decomposition dif fer in
parallel computing?
b) In which technique (domain or functional decomposition) are tasks divided
based on data, and in which are they divided based on functions?
c) Which decomposition technique is more suitable for problems with
independent data partitions, and which is better for problems with multiple
interdependent functions?05 CO4 BT4
 OR    
 B. Describe the basic structure of an MPI program with an example. 05 CO4 BT2
 
SECTION - B 
Q.1Answer the following questions. Marks CO BT
 A. (a)Explain the dif ference between static and dynamic job allocation
strategies.
(b) What is static job allocation?
(c) What is dynamic job allocation?06 CO5 BT1
 B. (a) Define the term Synchronization.
(b) what is its importance in parallel computing?
(c) What are common synchronization mechanisms used in parallel
computing?06 CO1 BT1
Q.2A. How can Amdahl’ s Law be used to estimate the minimum number of

 B. What is Scheduling Concept in parallel computing? 05CO1,
CO4BT1
 OR    
 B. How does error handling work in CUDA  programs? 05 CO2 BT1
Q.3A. Elaborate term thread and its attributes in detail with an example used in

 B. How does the DGX architecture improve upon traditional CPU-based
supercomputing systems?05 CO2 BT1
